seoTitle: Automate Scheduled Email & Teams Reminders in Power Automate
metaDescription: Build a recurring Power Automate flow that sends Outlook email reminders and Teams notifications from Excel, SharePoint, or Lists data.
intro: Sending reminder emails and Teams messages by hand for every deadline or approval is easy to forget. This guide shows how to build a scheduled Power Automate flow that automatically sends Outlook reminders and posts Teams notifications based on data pulled from Excel, SharePoint, or Microsoft Lists.

steps.heading: Create a scheduled cloud flow
steps.body: Start a new flow designed to run on a schedule rather than in response to a single event, so reminders go out consistently.

steps.heading: Configure the recurrence trigger
steps.body: Set how often the flow should run, such as daily or weekly, so it checks for upcoming tasks, approvals, or deadlines at the right cadence.

steps.heading: Retrieve reminder data
steps.body: Pull the relevant records from Excel, SharePoint, or Microsoft Lists so the flow knows who needs a reminder and what it's about.

steps.heading: Send Outlook emails and Teams notifications
steps.body: Use the retrieved data to send personalized Outlook emails and post messages to Microsoft Teams, adding conditions to avoid sending duplicate reminders.

faq.question: Can this send reminders through both email and Teams at once?
faq.answer: Yes, the same flow can include actions for sending an Outlook email and posting a Teams message so reminders reach people through both channels.

faq.question: How do I avoid sending the same reminder twice?
faq.answer: Add conditions in the flow, such as checking a status field, so the reminder only sends once per item rather than repeating on every run.

faq.question: Where can the reminder data come from?
faq.answer: The flow can pull data from Excel, SharePoint, or Microsoft Lists, depending on where your tasks, deadlines, or approvals are tracked.

faq.question: Can I personalize each reminder message?
faq.answer: Yes, dynamic content from the data source lets you insert specific names, dates, or task details into each reminder.
